modules.summary: Provides the sign-in interface that authenticates users with a username and password, granting access on valid credentials or displaying specific error banners for invalid or locked accounts.
modules.where_it_fits: This module is the application's entry point — users begin on the sign-in page and, upon successful authentication, are redirected to the Product Inventory page; it precedes all product browsing, cart, and checkout flows described in the navigation (Inventory → Product Detail → Shopping Cart → Checkout steps).
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is unauthenticated and has reached the sign-in page with credentials available to submit.

modules.summary: Displays the list of products with name, description, price, sorting options, and controls to add or remove items from the shopping cart.
modules.where_it_fits: This is the post-login landing page in the application flow (arrived at after sign-in) and features the persistent header with the hamburger menu (All Items, About, Logout, Reset App State) and cart badge. From here users can open Product Detail, modify cart contents which update the cart badge, and proceed into the Checkout sequence (Information → Overview → Confirmation).
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated user session (logged in) after successful sign-in.

modules.summary: Displays the selected product’s image, name, description, price, and a context-sensitive Add to cart / Remove button that reflects the current cart state.
modules.where_it_fits: Reached from Product Inventory by selecting a product (clicking its name or image), this page is part of the shopping flow and sits between browsing inventory and the Shopping Cart/checkout paths; the persistent header (hamburger menu and cart icon) remains available for navigation back to All Items, cart, or other actions.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in and has selected a specific product from the Product Inventory page.

modules.summary: Shows the list of items currently in the shopping cart with quantity, description, and per-item remove controls, and exposes actions to continue shopping or proceed to checkout.
modules.where_it_fits: Reached from the Product Inventory via the shopping cart icon or by navigating to the cart, the Shopping Cart is part of the purchase flow and leads into the Checkout steps (Checkout - Information → Overview → Confirmation); it is displayed within the app header/navigation structure (hamburger menu, title, cart badge).
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged-in user with one or more items previously added to the cart from Product Inventory.

modules.summary: Collects the buyer's first name, last name, and postal code and validates those required fields before advancing to the checkout overview.
modules.where_it_fits: This is the first step of the three-step checkout flow accessed from the Shopping Cart (Checkout - Information) as described in the app navigation; it precedes Checkout - Overview and Checkout - Confirmation. It is reached after adding items to the cart from the Product Inventory and entering the cart to begin checkout.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in with an active session and at least one item in the Shopping Cart initiating the checkout flow.

modules.summary: Displays an order summary of cart items with item total, tax, total, and shows the selected payment and shipping information while providing controls to finish or cancel the checkout.
modules.where_it_fits: This module is the second step in the three-step checkout flow reached from the Product Inventory via the Shopping Cart (Inventory → Cart → Checkout - Information → Checkout - Overview → Checkout - Confirmation). It appears after the Checkout - Information step and before the Checkout - Confirmation step in the purchase lifecycle.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in, cart contains one or more items, and checkout contact/payment/shipping information has been entered on the Checkout - Information step.

modules.summary: Displays the order success message and a Back Home action that returns the user to the Product Inventory and clears the cart.
modules.where_it_fits: This is the final step of the checkout flow (Information → Overview → Confirmation), reached after navigating from Product Inventory → Shopping Cart → Checkout - Information → Checkout - Overview. It completes the purchase lifecycle and returns the user to the inventory view.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in and has completed the checkout steps with an order submitted (checkout process reached the Confirmation step).

modules.summary: Terminates the user's session and returns the application to the login page, preventing access to protected pages until re-authentication.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the hamburger menu in the persistent header after login (e.g., from Product Inventory and other protected pages), Logout is a global action that ends the current session and returns the user to the sign-in page. It sits alongside other header menu items like All Items, About, and Reset App State as a session-control navigation option.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is currently authenticated and on a protected page (e.g., Product Inventory, Product Detail, Shopping Cart, or Checkout).

modules.summary: Resets the in-app state by clearing the shopping cart and restoring UI states (such as cart badge and add/remove button states) without signing the user out.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the persistent hamburger menu (item ④ Reset App State) available after signing in, this utility sits alongside navigation options like All Items, About, and Logout and is used during the shopping lifecycle to return the app to a clean state without affecting authentication. It is relevant while moving between Product Inventory, Product Detail, Shopping Cart, and Checkout flows when test or user state needs to be cleared.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is signed in with an active session and the application has a non-default state (e.g., items in cart or modified add/remove button states).
